name: Stuffed Flounder
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Chopped bell pepper, red or green
2 cups White bread torn into small pieces
1 1/4 lb Center slice boned Country ham, about 1/4 inch thick, finely diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Lay each fish bottom side (the white side) up on a cutting board and make an incision in each fish down to the backbone along the center, stopping just shy of the head and tail.
3. Working at an oblique angle, slip the knife between the flesh and backbone and run it down the ribs on both sides of the backbone.
4. In a heavy sauté pan, heat about half of the butter over medium-high heat. Add the onion, celery, and bell pepper; cook until the onions become transparent, about 5 minutes.
5. Toss the vegetables with crabmeat, bread, eggs, parsley, ham, and sherry. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Remember that the country ham is salty and will impart its flavor to the crabmeat.
6. Stuff each fish with the mixture.
7. Brush a glass baking pan with some of the remaining butter.
8. Add the stuffed fish to the pan.
9. Mix the rest of the butter with lemon juice; pour over the fish.
10. Bake for 30-45 minutes, or until the fish flakes easily with a fork.
